content:
I feel like most stages in arc 1 normal mode got hit a bit too hard with the new nerf. Some hp values in chapter 8 are similar or just lower than chapter 4-5 bosses which feels really weird. I think it would also be a problem for players who would finish arc 1 and enter arc 2 where enemies have astronomically higher numbers (and i really wouldn't suggest nerfing arc 2 content as well), or even just madness stages of events or higher alerts in D-Tides.
I think that before the main story's content had a better flow with its difficulty, without being unfair in the slightest. I wouldn't say the game needs to revert the nerf but it should be in my opinion toned down.

I requested this once previously, so I will be copy and pasting some of the previous post, as the problems are still present.

The auto-scroll in the English language is incredibly disruptive and is severely hurting my experience with the game. The biggest part of this game's appeal is its story, but its story is being hindered by a poor auto-scroll feature that is causing people, myself included, to be pulled out of the story and break immersion. For me personally, I have had the recent event dialogues totally ruined by being frustrated at the poor auto-scroll completely disrupting the flow of the story. Below are three examples of why the auto-scroll is a problem.

Scenario 1: The text often scrolls before the player is given time to properly take in what has been said, requiring the player to check the logs to re-read it and breaking immersion.

Scenario 2: The game does not allow you to proceed to the next dialogue box until the scroll has finished. This becomes a problem when simple, easy to understand sentences are written and you want to advance to the next dialogue window but have to wait for the scroll to finish in order to do so.

Scenario 3: Because there is no instantaneous text display, people like me who are used to faster text speeds click once to have the entire dialogue box filled immediately. With auto-scroll, it now becomes a guess whether I am about to skip dialogue or not because of scrolling text when I do this. 

All three of these scenarios are very obnoxious and have a negative impact on the experience of players and were not a problem with the old system of spilling over into a new dialogue window. I know that I am not the only player bothered by this, I have seen several others complain about this feature; including players new to the game who hadn't played using the old system. Please revert this change to maintain the immersion and player enjoyment of the story.

I am about at my wit's end with this auto-scroll ruining my experience.

Just some feedback and thoughts I have. 
I don't usually play gacha games because I find most of them being too predatory for customers despite me liking the general concept, and Morimens was one of those rare gacha games I could play without thinking about how the developers try to psychologically affect my actions.
That being said the new update changed the store layout and now every time you enter it you need to first go through the cash shop before you can even access the Exchange store-
Even if that exposure is not predatory enough for an average player I still think its just increadibly annoying to click through several tabs just to get to the store page you need especially when you need to visit it multiple times
thread_title: New store update feels predatory and it sucks

content:
I love the changes to 👻 dive. The replay and watch feature is amazing.

I think a way to make it better is if players could click thier rank (example, I would click 298) and it would bring me to my spot on the leaderboard.

Then I could scroll up and see players a few ranks above me and what team they used/maybe a better way to play it at my level.

Some feedback players are having is its great to see the top players' replays, but it would be nice to see players near your rank and their replays too.

Thanks!
